question: A child process referenced by a call activity defines a none start event and a message start event. When the call activity is activated, which start event instantiates the child instance?

options.text: The none start event; any other start event types in the child process are ignored.
The message start event, because messages take priority over a none start event.
Both start events are activated in parallel, creating two execution branches in the child.
The first start event in the BPMN XML order, regardless of type.

a.text: A call activity always activates the child at its none start event; the docs explicitly state that other start event types in the called process are ignored.

b.text: Message start events are used for message-correlated process instantiation, not by a call activity. The call path always targets the none start event.

c.text: Only one start event runs when a call activity instantiates the child — the none start event. Other start events do not fan out a second branch.

d.text: Selection is by start-event type (none), not by XML declaration order. Even if the message start event appears first, it is ignored.

explanation: When a call activity invokes another process, a new instance of the referenced process is created and activated at its none start event. The process may declare other start event types, but those are ignored on this invocation path.
